body_text: commit moified files so far Committed as `9a2fae8` on `main` — frontend.py and test_frontend.py changes bundled into one commit covering the depth-aware link fix, stale-output cleanup, and home-page wiki preview. superpowers:brainstorm /superpowers:brainstorm make our frontend website beautiful, useful and meaningful. current website is hard to see its purpose and ability. i want a real LLM Wiki which evolves as new data comes or codebase changes in terms of documents or code structure. i don't need each code function's relationship. it's too microscopic and means nothing in this AI era. we don't see every single code line. we have data/ folder which is raw documents. so whenever ingest occurs this project automatically go through all the updatedd docs and files and relate them, and keep updating higher layer's knowledge by writing docs of it, and so on. this kind of knowledgebase is reflected to the website. some examples i want to mimic are: - https://github.com/nashsu/llm_wiki - https://github.com/lucasastorian/llmwiki - https://github.com/Pratiyush/llm-wiki mix all the websites above by getting good design and ui/ux and functionalities into our website. make our website product

python3 -c "
from pathlib import Path
import tempfile
from llm_wiki.site import StaticSiteBuilder
from llm_wiki.research_graph import ResearchEdge, ResearchGraph, ResearchNode, ResearchNodeType
from llm_wiki.wiki_store import WikiPageStore, WikiPage

g = ResearchGraph(
    nodes=[
        ResearchNode(id='Paper:demo', name='Demo Paper', type=ResearchNodeType.PAPER),
        ResearchNode(id='Concept:gs', name='Gaussian Splatting', type=ResearchNodeType.CONCEPT),
    ],
    edges=[ResearchEdge(source='Paper:demo', target='Concept:gs', type='mentioned_in')],
)
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    td = Path(td)
    wiki = td / 'wiki'
    wiki.mkdir()
    (wiki/'papers').mkdir()
    (wiki/'papers'/'demo-paper.md').write_text('---\ntitle: Demo Paper\nsource_path: data/demo.pdf\n---\n# Demo Paper\n\nAbout splatting.\n\n## Methods\n- Gaussians\n', encoding='utf-8')

    out1 = td / 'site1'
    out2 = td / 'site2'
    StaticSiteBuilder('TestWiki').write_site(g, wiki, out1)
    StaticSiteBuilder('TestWiki').write_site(g, wiki, out2)

    files1 = {p.relative_to(out1): p.read_bytes() for p in out1.rglob('*') if p.is_file()}
    files2 = {p.relative_to(out2): p.read_bytes() for p in out2.rglob('*') if p.is_file()}
    print('files1:', len(files1), 'files2:', len(files2))
    if set(files1.keys()) != set(files2.keys()):
        print('KEY DIFF', set(files1)^set(files2))
    for k in sorted(files1):
        if files1[k] != files2[k]:
            print('DIFF:', k)
    print('OK if no DIFF')
" 2>&1 | head -30

  ``ExportContext``; derive RSS ``<lastBuildDate>`` and per-item
  ``<pubDate>`` from the synthesis ledger; fall back to the Unix epoch
  when the ledger is empty (stable, clearly bogus).

  the output dir, parse the synthesis ledger when wiki_root is set,
  thread it into the export context, and append a single
  ``.build-history.jsonl`` entry per build (file_count + total_bytes +
  built_at). ``manifest.json`` now carries ``version: "1"`` and
  ``generator: "llm-wiki"`` only — no top-level ``built_at``.

  twice over the wiki_corpus fixture and asserts byte-equality across
  ``.llm-wiki/site/`` and ``.llm-wiki/wiki/`` (excluding the two
  ledgers); ``test_synthesis.py`` adds a byte-equality assertion using
  the production ``WikiPageStore``; ``test_frontend.py`` updated to

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Fix extraction: stop minting concepts from headings, promote arxiv links to Papers

The previous extractor minted a Concept node for every markdown heading in
SourceDocument files, which polluted the Concepts index with paper-title
fragments from daily digests (e.g. "1. 생성 모델이 분할과 깊이 추정까지 넘본다,
Vision Banana"). Arxiv links inside digest bodies were also dropped on the
floor instead of being promoted to Paper nodes.

This change:
* classifies headings before committing them — generic section names
  ("Introduction", "Results"…) are skipped, paper-title-shaped headings
  go into ``metadata["candidate_paper_titles"]``, and only short
  noun-phrase headings that match a whitelist token or a TermRule
  canonical name survive as Concept nodes (capped at 6 per file);
* adds ``_extract_paper_references`` which scans for arxiv URLs, bare
  ``arXiv:NNNN.NNNNN`` references, and ``papers/<id>/paper.md`` paths
  in document bodies and mints one Paper node per id with a
  ``mentioned_in`` edge from the source document;
* makes ``source_kind_to_node_type`` path-precise for daily-digest
  layouts (``data/research/.+?/papers/<id>/paper.md`` → Paper,
  ``/repo.md`` or ``/repos/`` → Repository, ``/feeds/`` → SourceDocument);
* introduces ``id_seed`` on ``ResearchGraphBuilder.add_node`` so the
  Paper node id is derived from ``arXiv:<id>`` (stable across digest
  mentions and per-paper file ingest) while the display name remains
  the human title;
* ships ``tests/test_extraction_quality.py`` with golden assertions on
  the digest-vs-paper-file collision case and a corpus-level invariant
  (Papers > Concepts on the bundled fixture corpus).

On the project's ``data/`` corpus (651 markdown files), the bug-driven
ratio flips from Papers=98 / Concepts=378 to Papers=180 / Concepts=14.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -10

Optional `LLM_WIKI_SYNTHESIS_LLM=1` upgrade replaces the deterministic
heuristic templates with prompted Claude prose. The heuristic stays the
default ship and the always-available fallback; the LLM path activates
only when the env var, an Anthropic API key, and the SDK import all line
up. Per-page failures (empty body, missing citations, network errors,
rate limits) fall back to the heuristic for that page only with one
deduped stderr log per error class.

`cache_control: ephemeral` block so every page after the first hits the
prompt cache. `LLM_WIKI_SYNTHESIS_DRY_RUN=1` exercises prompt
construction and emits stub bodies without spending tokens.

The `anthropic` SDK is an optional extra (`pip install
llm-wiki[synthesis-llm]`) — the base install keeps working and the
import is lazy.
